summary: Verified the iter-28 'Verifier Config' refactor end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 pytest cases pass against the new /api/eval/verifier-config?bench=<bench> GET/PUT/POST endpoints — including per-bench required-token validation ({golden}+{candidate} vs {preview_url}+{test_case}), independent persistence (saving scratch does not touch testing_agent and vice versa), legacy /api/eval/judge-config alias still returns judge_prompt/judge_model and writes through to bench=testing_agent_bench, and the unknown-bench path returns 400 with both allowed values in detail. Default scratch prompt is 1231 chars and contains both required tokens; default model is 'gemini-flash-latest'. Frontend: sidebar entry now reads 'Verifier Config' (href=/judge-config, Scale icon retained); /judge-config renders [data-testid='verifier-config-page'] with a bench dropdown [data-testid='verifier-bench-select'] that has EXACTLY two options ('Testing Agent Bench','Scratch Bench'); switching benches loads independent configs (verified by saving custom prompts on each side and confirming neither leaks across); model select shows EXACTLY three options ['gemini-flash-latest','gpt-5.5','Custom…'] and Custom… reveals the free-text input; per-bench validation correctly disables Save when {golden} (TA) or {preview_url} (scratch) is removed; token chips show ✓/✗ states. Integration via Playwright network interception of POST /api/eval/jobs: phase A (scratch verifier is_default=true, 2 scratch_bench_phased datasets) — every eval in the batched payload OMITS experiments.browser_prompt/browser_model; phase B (custom scratch verifier saved) — every eval in the batched payload has experiments.browser_prompt==<saved prompt> AND experiments.browser_model==<saved model>. Verdict footer helper text appears on scratch ('verdict format is appended automatically by the harness').
action_items: Minor doc-only nit: the review request copy uses the literal URL 'https://replay-browser-tests.preview.emergentagent.com' as a required token, but the actual implementation correctly uses the {preview_url} substitution placeholder (validated in backend and frontend). Update the request wording to '{preview_url}' to avoid future confusion.
critical_code_review_comments: BENCH_VERIFIER_DEFAULTS + _validate_verifier_prompt + _read_verifier_doc cleanly generalize the singleton config — legacy alias endpoints are thin shims, no code duplication.

_read_verifier_doc.fallback to _id='default' for testing_agent_bench preserves pre-iter-28 customizations on first read — but the new PUT writes under _id='testing_agent_bench' without migrating/deleting the old doc. If a user later POSTs reset, only _id='testing_agent_bench' AND _id='default' are dropped (which is correct), but until the user saves once they continue reading from _id='default' on every request — a one-shot migration on first read would simplify reasoning. Non-blocking.
RunEvalModal.js lazily fetches scratchVerifier only when selectedProblems contains a scratch_bench_phased entry — correct. The is_default guard in handleSubmit gates both stamping paths consistently.

RunEvalModal.js is 1436 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Extracting Step1/Step2/Step3 + the bench-aware verifier wiring into focused submodules would simplify future iterations. Non-blocking but recommended.
JudgeConfigDialog.js cleanly exports both new (VerifierConfigForm/Dialog, BENCH_META) and back-compat (JudgeConfigForm/Dialog) names — minimal churn for callers.
seed_data_creation: None. The autouse pytest fixture resets BOTH bench docs before+after every test, so the DB is left identical to its pre-test state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_verifier_config.py (13 tests). Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) python -m pytest backend/tests/test_verifier_config.py -v. For frontend RunEvalModal flow, the 'New Eval' button is labelled 'New Eval' (NOT 'Run Eval'). Group Run ID on Step 2 is REQUIRED — Next button stays disabled until filled. Scratch datasets used: scratch_bench_phased/ecom_app_structured and scratch_bench_phased/ecommerce_app. To intercept the batched POST, route '**/api/eval/jobs**' and skip URLs that contain 'verifier' (the verifier-config PUT also matches the glob).
rca of the issue: n/a — no bug found. The refactor is functional, backwards-compatible, and the is_default gating correctly prevents stamping when the user hasn't customized either bench.
